policy_statement: The Government should provide a mechanism defining when and on what basis a decision will be made on the back-end of the nuclear fuel cycle.
verbatim_text: R1 Recommendation: The Government should provide the mechanism for when and on what basis the decision shall be made on the back-end of the nuclear fuel cycle.
sector: Nuclear / Radioactive Waste & Spent Fuel Management
extraction_rationale: Explicit recommended government action to establish a decision-making mechanism for spent fuel back-end; concrete governance intervention.
reasoning: This statement recommends that the Government “provide the mechanism” to define *when* and *on what basis* a decision will be made about the back-end of the nuclear fuel cycle. It is a governance/process recommendation rather than an auditable, measurable deliverable with a deadline. The text contains no quantified outcome (e.g., number of facilities, % of waste processed, capacity targets) and no explicit timeline/year by which the mechanism must be established. Language is advisory (“should”) and does not describe an enforceable mandate or legal instrument.
validation_reasoning: AUDITABLE COMMITMENT: The text calls for a process/governance mechanism rather than a measurable outcome: "should provide the mechanism for when and on what basis the decision shall be made". QUANTIFIABLE TARGET: No numeric/measurable outcome is stated (no %, MW, volumes, facilities, etc.). TIMELINE: No explicit deadline/year is provided; "when" is not specified as a date. BINDING MECHANISM: Uses advisory language "should" and does not cite an ordinance, mandate, regulation, or code change. SPATIAL SPECIFICITY: No location/jurisdiction is specified beyond generic "The Government". LANGUAGE STRENGTH: Weak language present ("should provide"), and no hard numbers; thus weak_language_detected=Yes and strong_language_detected=No. Under the strict rule, lacking both timeline and quantifiable target makes it NON-SOUND; confidence capped accordingly.

extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory development action assigned to the regulator (HAEA), explicitly covering VLLW management including disposal.
reasoning: The statement is a non-binding suggestion (“should consider”) for HAEA to complete development of safety regulations for very low activity level radioactive waste. While it identifies a concrete policy area (regulations for management and disposal), it lacks (1) an explicit deadline/year and (2) any measurable outcome/target. The language is weak and does not constitute an enforceable commitment, so it fails the strict validity rule.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: No—text contains no measurable outcome (no % reduction, number of facilities, compliance rate, etc.); it only says "consider completing development of safety regulations". Timeline: No—no date/deadline/year is provided. Binding mechanism: No—the phrasing is advisory: "HAEA should consider" and does not indicate a mandate, enacted regulation, or enforceable requirement. Spatial specificity: No—no jurisdictional scope (e.g., national, citywide, facility-specific) is stated in the excerpt. Weak language: Yes—"should consider" indicates a suggestion rather than commitment. Strong language: No—no 'require/mandate' phrasing with hard numbers. Therefore it fails the strict rule (missing both timeline and quantifiable target; weak language with no binding mechanism).

policy_statement: The Government should consider approving an updated national program without undue delay, taking into account the Paks II project and other relevant technical circumstances.

extraction_rationale: Concrete governance action to approve an updated national programme with specified considerations (Paks II and technical circumstances).
reasoning: This statement is advisory (“should consider”) and focuses on a procedural action (consider approving an updated national program) rather than an auditable, measurable climate outcome. It contains no explicit deadline/year and no quantifiable target. While it references the Paks II project, it does not specify binding legal force, geographic scope beyond being implicitly national, or measurable deliverables.
validation_reasoning:
AUDITABLE COMMITMENT: The text is a suggestion and framed as consideration: "should consider approving an updated national program"—this is a process action with no concrete, auditable delivery metrics.
TIMELINE: No explicit date/year; "without undue delay" is vague and does not qualify as a timeline.
QUANTIFIABLE TARGET: No measurable outcome (no MW, %, units, etc.). References to "Paks II project" and "technical circumstances" do not provide numeric targets.
BINDING MECHANISM: No enforceable mechanism (no law/mandate/requirement); it is explicitly labeled "Suggestion" and uses non-binding phrasing.
SPATIAL SPECIFICITY: "national program" implies a national scope but does not explicitly state where it applies; no explicit geographic qualifier is provided in the text.
LANGUAGE STRENGTH: Weak language is present ("should consider"); no strong mandatory verbs with hard numbers.
STRICT VALID RULE: Fails because has_timeline='No' and has_quantifiable_target='No', and language is weak without binding mechanism.
